parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Develop, document, and disseminate access control policies and procedures.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Create or update the access control policy document and ensure it is reviewed annually.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Review AWS Organizations SCPs: aws organizations list-policies --filter SERVICE_CONTROL_POLICY
- Check IAM password policy: aws iam get-account-password-policy
- Verify policies are documented in your GRC system and reviewed within the last 12 months

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify access control policy exists and is current

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Manage system accounts including establishing, activating, modifying, reviewing, disabling, and removing accounts.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Ensure all IAM users have MFA enabled and root access keys are removed.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Run: aws iam generate-credential-report && aws iam get-credential-report --output text --query Content | base64 -d to audit all users
- For each user without MFA: aws iam create-virtual-mfa-device --virtual-mfa-device-name <user>-mfa --outfile qr.png
- Enable MFA: aws iam enable-mfa-device --user-name <user> --serial-number <arn> --authentication-code1 <code1> --authentication-code2 <code2>
- For privileged accounts, use hardware tokens registered via IAM console under Security Credentials
- Add IAM policy condition aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent to enforce MFA for sensitive operations
- Create AWS Config rule iam-user-mfa-enabled for continuous monitoring

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose:
- Navigate to Entra ID > Security > MFA > Getting started to configure MFA policies
- Create a Conditional Access policy requiring MFA for all users
- Use az ad user list --query '[?!strongAuthenticationDetail]' to find users without MFA

parts.name: remediation-gcp
parts.prose:
- Enable 2-Step Verification enforcement in Google Workspace Admin > Security > 2-Step Verification
- Use gcloud identity groups memberships list to audit group-based access

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Identify and select account types

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Replace overly permissive IAM policies with scoped, least-privilege policies.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Identify attached policies: aws iam list-attached-user-policies --user-name <user>
- Detach AdministratorAccess: aws iam detach-user-policy --user-name <user> --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AdministratorAccess
- Generate least-privilege policy via IAM Access Analyzer: aws accessanalyzer start-policy-generation
- Create scoped policy and attach: aws iam put-user-policy or aws iam attach-user-policy
- Migrate users to IAM roles with session duration limits: aws iam create-role --max-session-duration 3600
- Audit activity via CloudTrail to verify correct permissions during transition

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Specify authorized users and access authorizations

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Enforce approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Ensure IAM policies enforce approved access and deny by default.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Review IAM policies for explicit deny statements: aws iam get-policy-version --policy-arn <arn> --version-id <v>
- Verify S3 bucket policies block public access: aws s3api get-public-access-block --bucket <bucket>
- Check for SCP guardrails: aws organizations list-policies-for-target --target-id <ou-id> --filter SERVICE_CONTROL_POLICY

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify access enforcement mechanisms are in place

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Enforce approved authorizations for controlling the flow of information within the system and between systems.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement VPC flow logs and security group rules to control data flow between tiers.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable VPC Flow Logs: aws ec2 create-flow-logs --resource-type VPC --resource-ids <vpc-id> --traffic-type ALL --log-destination-type cloud-watch-logs --log-group-name vpc-flow-logs
- Review security group rules: aws ec2 describe-security-groups --filters Name=vpc-id,Values=<vpc-id>
- Implement network ACLs for subnet-level controls: aws ec2 create-network-acl-entry

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify information flow controls

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Separate duties of individuals to prevent malicious activity and reduce errors.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement IAM permission boundaries and SCPs to enforce separation of duties.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create permission boundaries: aws iam create-policy --policy-name dev-boundary --policy-document file://boundary.json
- Attach boundary to users: aws iam put-user-permissions-boundary --user-name <user> --permissions-boundary <arn>
- Ensure no single user has both billing and IAM admin: review with aws iam list-attached-user-policies

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify separation of duties is defined and enforced

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Employ the principle of least privilege, allowing only authorized accesses necessary to accomplish assigned tasks.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Remove wildcard admin policies and disable unused credentials.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List custom policies: aws iam list-policies --scope Local --query 'Policies[?contains(PolicyName,`Admin`) || contains(PolicyName,`FullAccess`)]'
- Get policy document: aws iam get-policy-version --policy-arn <arn> --version-id <v> to inspect for Action:* or Resource:*
- Replace with scoped policies using IAM Access Analyzer policy generation
- Disable unused credentials: aws iam update-access-key --access-key-id <key> --status Inactive --user-name <user>
- Delete unused login profiles: aws iam delete-login-profile --user-name <user>
- Set up AWS Config rule iam-user-unused-credentials-check with maxCredentialUsageAge=90

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Authorize access for security functions

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Enforce a limit of consecutive invalid logon attempts by a user.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Configure account lockout via IAM password policy and Cognito/SSO settings.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Review password policy: aws iam get-account-password-policy
- For SSO: configure lockout in IAM Identity Center Settings
- For Cognito: aws cognito-idp update-user-pool --user-pool-id <id> --policies '{"PasswordPolicy":{"MinimumLength":14}}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify lockout policy after failed logon attempts

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Display system use notification message or banner before granting access to the system that provides privacy and security notices consistent with applicable laws, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and guidelines.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Configure login banners on all systems and services to display approved system use notification messages.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List managed instances: aws ssm describe-instance-information --query 'InstanceInformationList[].InstanceId'
- Configure login banner via SSM: aws ssm send-command --targets Key=InstanceIds,Values=<id> --document-name AWS-RunShellScript --parameters commands=['echo "Authorized users only. All activity is monitored." > /etc/issue']
- Configure SSH banner: aws ssm send-command --targets Key=InstanceIds,Values=<id> --document-name AWS-RunShellScript --parameters commands=['echo Banner /etc/issue >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config && systemctl restart sshd']
- For AWS Console, configure a custom sign-in banner via AWS IAM Identity Center: aws sso-admin list-instances

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify system use notification banner is displayed before granting access

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Notify the user, upon successful logon, of the date and time of the last logon, the number of unsuccessful logon attempts since the last successful logon, and any changes to the account since the last logon.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable previous logon notification on systems and review CloudTrail for console sign-in events.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Check last console login: aws iam get-user --user-name <username> --query 'User.PasswordLastUsed'
- Query recent login events: aws cloudtrail lookup-events --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=EventName,AttributeValue=ConsoleLogin --max-results 10
- Enable pam_lastlog on instances: aws ssm send-command --targets Key=InstanceIds,Values=<id> --document-name AWS-RunShellScript --parameters commands=['pam-auth-update --enable lastlog']

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify system displays previous logon information upon successful authentication

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Limit the number of concurrent sessions for each system account to a defined number of sessions.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Configure concurrent session limits via IAM policies and instance-level controls.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create IAM policy to restrict concurrent sessions: aws iam create-policy --policy-name ConcurrentSessionLimit --policy-document file://concurrent-session-policy.json
- Configure SSO session duration: aws sso-admin update-instance-access-control-attribute-configuration --instance-arn <arn>
- Set session limits on instances: aws ssm send-command --targets Key=InstanceIds,Values=<id> --document-name AWS-RunShellScript --parameters commands=['echo "* hard maxlogins 2" >> /etc/security/limits.conf']
- Verify STS session token duration: aws sts get-session-token --duration-seconds 3600

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify concurrent session limits are enforced

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Prevent further access to the system by initiating a device lock after a defined inactivity period.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Configure session timeouts on console and CLI access.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Set console session duration: via IAM Role trust policy with MaxSessionDuration
- Configure SSO session timeout: IAM Identity Center → Settings → Session duration
- For CLI: configure credential expiry in ~/.aws/config with duration_seconds

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify session lock after inactivity

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Automatically terminate a user session after a defined period of inactivity or when conditions require session disconnect.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Configure session timeout policies across all AWS services and managed instances.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Check IAM role maximum session duration: aws iam get-role --role-name <role-name> --query 'Role.MaxSessionDuration'
- Configure SSO session timeout: aws sso-admin update-instance-access-control-attribute-configuration --instance-arn <arn>
- Set SSH timeout on instances: aws ssm send-command --targets Key=InstanceIds,Values=<id> --document-name AWS-RunShellScript --parameters commands=['echo "ClientAliveInterval 300" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config && echo "ClientAliveCountMax 0" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config && systemctl restart sshd']
- Verify Console session duration: aws iam list-roles --query 'Roles[].MaxSessionDuration'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify automatic session termination is configured for inactive sessions

controls.title: Permitted Actions Without Identification or Authentication

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Identify specific user actions that can be performed on the system without identification or authentication consistent with organizational mission and business functions.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Review and restrict public access to AWS resources, ensuring only intentional unauthenticated actions are permitted.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Check S3 public access settings: aws s3control get-public-access-block --account-id <account-id>
- List public S3 buckets: aws s3api list-buckets --query 'Buckets[].Name' | xargs -I {} aws s3api get-bucket-policy-status --bucket {}
- Check for public API Gateway endpoints: aws apigateway get-rest-apis --query 'items[?endpointConfiguration.types[0]==`EDGE`]'
- Review CloudFront distributions for unauthenticated access: aws cloudfront list-distributions --query 'DistributionList.Items[].{Id:Id,Origins:Origins}'
- Check for public Lambda function URLs: aws lambda list-function-url-configs --function-name <name>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify that only approved actions are permitted without authentication

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Provide the means to associate security and privacy attributes with information in storage, in process, and in transmission, and ensure that the attributes are maintained with the information.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement resource tagging policies and data classification labels across AWS resources.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List tag policies: aws organizations list-policies --filter TAG_POLICY
- Check resource tagging compliance: aws resourcegroupstaggingapi get-resources --tag-filters Key=Classification
- Create a tag policy for data classification: aws organizations create-policy --name DataClassification --type TAG_POLICY --content file://tag-policy.json
- Enable AWS Config rule required-tags: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule file://required-tags-rule.json
- Check Macie classification: aws macie2 list-findings --finding-criteria '{"criterion":{"classificationDetails.result.sensitiveData.category":{"exists":true}}}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify security attributes are associated with information

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Establish and document usage restrictions and implementation guidance for remote access.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Use SSM Session Manager instead of direct SSH. Enforce VPN or PrivateLink for API access.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable SSM Session Manager: attach AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore policy to EC2 instance roles
- Close SSH (port 22) in security groups: aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress --group-id <sg-id> --protocol tcp --port 22 --cidr 0.0.0.0/0
- Create VPC endpoints for private API access: aws ec2 create-vpc-endpoint --vpc-id <vpc-id> --service-name com.amazonaws.<region>.ssm
- Enable CloudTrail logging for Session Manager: already included in management events

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify remote access controls are implemented

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Establish and document usage restrictions and implementation guidance for wireless access.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Ensure corporate wireless uses WPA3/Enterprise and is segmented from production networks.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- For cloud workloads, wireless is N/A — document this in SSP
- For corporate offices using AWS networking: segment wireless via separate VPC/subnet

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify wireless access restrictions

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Establish and document usage restrictions for mobile devices.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enforce MDM enrollment and conditional access policies for mobile device access.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Use AWS WorkSpaces for managed mobile access: aws workspaces create-workspaces
- Configure conditional access via IAM Identity Center policies

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify mobile device management controls

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Establish terms and conditions for authorized individuals to access the system from external systems.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Document approved external systems and enforce access via IAM conditions.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Add IP-based conditions to IAM policies: aws:SourceIp condition key
- Use VPC endpoints to restrict API access to known networks
- Implement SCPs to deny actions from outside approved regions: aws:RequestedRegion

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify external system access restrictions

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Employ automated mechanisms or manual processes to assist users in making information sharing and collaboration decisions, and enforce access authorizations to support information sharing.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Configure cross-account access controls, resource sharing policies, and data sharing agreements in AWS.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List RAM resource shares: aws ram get-resource-shares --resource-owner SELF
- Review cross-account IAM roles: aws iam list-roles --query 'Roles[?AssumeRolePolicyDocument.Statement[?Principal.AWS]]'
- Check S3 bucket policies for cross-account access: aws s3api get-bucket-policy --bucket <bucket-name>
- List Lake Formation permissions: aws lakeformation list-permissions
- Review KMS key policies for cross-account grants: aws kms list-grants --key-id <key-id>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify information sharing controls and authorization mechanisms are in place

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Designate individuals authorized to post information onto publicly accessible systems.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable S3 Block Public Access at the account level and audit CloudFront distributions.

parts.name: remediation-aws
assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify controls over publicly accessible content

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Employ data mining prevention and detection techniques for organization-defined data storage objects to detect and protect against unauthorized data mining.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable Amazon Macie for sensitive data discovery and configure GuardDuty for anomalous data access detection.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable Macie: aws macie2 enable-macie
- Create Macie classification job: aws macie2 create-classification-job --job-type SCHEDULED --s3-job-definition '{"bucketDefinitions":[{"accountId":"<account-id>","buckets":["<bucket>"]}]}' --name DataMiningDetection
- Enable GuardDuty: aws guardduty create-detector --enable
- Check for anomalous API activity: aws guardduty list-findings --detector-id <id> --finding-criteria '{"criterion":{"type":{"eq":["Recon:IAMUser/TorIPCaller","UnauthorizedAccess:IAMUser/InstanceCredentialExfiltration.OutsideAWS"]}}}'
- Configure VPC Flow Logs for network-level data exfiltration detection: aws ec2 create-flow-logs --resource-type VPC --resource-ids <vpc-id> --traffic-type ALL --log-destination-type s3 --log-destination <bucket-arn>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify data mining prevention and detection controls are implemented

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Establish procedures to ensure access control decisions are applied to each access request prior to access enforcement, using a reference monitor or equivalent mechanism.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement centralized policy evaluation using IAM Access Analyzer, SCPs, and permission boundaries.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List SCPs: aws organizations list-policies --filter SERVICE_CONTROL_POLICY
- Verify SCP enforcement: aws organizations describe-policy --policy-id <policy-id>
- Check IAM policy simulator for access decisions: aws iam simulate-principal-policy --policy-source-arn <arn> --action-names s3:GetObject ec2:RunInstances
- List permission boundaries: aws iam list-users --query 'Users[].PermissionsBoundary'
- Enable IAM Access Analyzer: aws accessanalyzer create-analyzer --analyzer-name OrgAnalyzer --type ORGANIZATION

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify access control decisions are enforced for each access request

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement a reference monitor for organization-defined access control policies that is tamperproof, always invoked, and small enough to be subject to analysis and testing.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Ensure AWS IAM and SCPs act as the reference monitor, with CloudTrail logging all access decisions.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Verify CloudTrail is enabled in all regions: aws cloudtrail describe-trails --query 'trailList[].{Name:Name,IsMultiRegion:IsMultiRegionTrail,IsLogging:IsLogging}'
- Check SCP enforcement at organization root: aws organizations list-policies-for-target --target-id <root-id> --filter SERVICE_CONTROL_POLICY
- Verify IAM policy evaluation logging: aws cloudtrail lookup-events --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=EventName,AttributeValue=AssumeRole --max-results 5
- Ensure AWS Config is recording: aws configservice describe-configuration-recorder-status

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify reference monitor mechanism is implemented and always invoked

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Develop, document, and disseminate awareness and training policy and procedures.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Create a security awareness training policy with annual review cadence.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Document training policy requirements in your GRC system
- Track completion via AWS SSO groups or training platform integration

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify security awareness training policy exists

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Provide security literacy training to system users, including awareness of security risks.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Deploy annual security awareness training and track completion.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Use AWS Training and Certification for cloud security training
- Track completion via LMS integration or manual CSV uploads
- Create AWS Config custom rule to verify training tag on IAM users

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify security awareness training is provided to all users

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Provide role-based security training to personnel with assigned security roles.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Ensure privileged users receive additional security training specific to their roles.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Identify privileged users: aws iam list-users then check attached policies
- Ensure admin/security team members complete AWS Security Specialty training
- Track role-based training completion per NIST requirements

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify role-based training is provided

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Document and monitor security training activities including completion.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Maintain training records with dates, topics, and completion status.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Export training completion data from LMS
- Store records in compliance-tagged S3 bucket with versioning enabled

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify training records are maintained

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Provide feedback on organizational training results to designated personnel at a defined frequency.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Configure automated training compliance reporting and feedback collection.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Check IAM credential report for user training compliance: aws iam generate-credential-report && aws iam get-credential-report --output text --query Content | base64 -d
- Review Security Hub findings related to user access: aws securityhub get-findings --filters '{"Type":[{"Value":"Software and Configuration Checks/AWS Security Best Practices","Comparison":"PREFIX"}]}' --max-results 10
- Use SNS to send training reminders: aws sns publish --topic-arn <arn> --message 'Security training feedback required' --subject 'Training Feedback Request'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify training feedback mechanisms are in place and results are reported

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Develop, document, and disseminate audit and accountability policy and procedures.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Create audit and accountability policy defining logging requirements.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Document audit policy covering CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and VPC Flow Logs requirements
- Verify CloudTrail is enabled: aws cloudtrail describe-trails

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify audit policy exists

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Identify events that the system is capable of logging in support of the audit function.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable multi-region CloudTrail with log file validation and delivery to a secured S3 bucket.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create multi-region trail: aws cloudtrail create-trail --name org-trail --s3-bucket-name <bucket> --is-multi-region-trail --enable-log-file-validation
- Start logging: aws cloudtrail start-logging --name org-trail
- Verify: aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name org-trail (check IsLogging=true)
- Enable data events for S3 and Lambda: aws cloudtrail put-event-selectors --trail-name org-trail --event-selectors '[{"DataResources":[{"Type":"AWS::S3::Object","Values":["arn:aws:s3"]}]}]'
- Configure log delivery to CloudWatch: aws cloudtrail update-trail --name org-trail --cloud-watch-logs-log-group-arn <arn> --cloud-watch-logs-role-arn <role-arn>

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Enable diagnostic settings: az monitor diagnostic-settings create --name audit-logs --resource <id> --logs '[{"category":"AuditEvent","enabled":true}]'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Identify event types for logging

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Ensure audit records contain information needed to establish what events occurred.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Verify CloudTrail records include who, what, when, where, and outcome fields.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Check trail configuration: aws cloudtrail get-trail --name <trail>
- Verify event selectors include management and data events
- CloudTrail records automatically include: eventTime, eventSource, eventName, userIdentity, sourceIPAddress, responseElements

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify audit records contain required fields

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Allocate audit log storage capacity to accommodate anticipated log volume.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Configure S3 lifecycle policies for log retention and ensure bucket has no size limits.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Check CloudTrail S3 bucket: aws s3api get-bucket-lifecycle-configuration --bucket <trail-bucket>
- Configure lifecycle rules for log tiering: aws s3api put-bucket-lifecycle-configuration
- Set up CloudWatch alarms for S3 bucket size: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify sufficient log storage capacity

controls.title: Response to Audit Logging Process Failures

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Alert personnel in the event of an audit logging process failure.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Configure CloudWatch alarms and SNS notifications for CloudTrail delivery failures.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create CloudWatch metric filter for CloudTrail failures
- Create alarm: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name cloudtrail-failure --metric-name DeliveryErrors
- Configure SNS notification: aws sns create-topic --name audit-failures

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify alerting on audit logging failures

controls.title: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Review and analyze system audit records for indications of inappropriate or unusual activity.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable GuardDuty for threat detection and Security Hub for centralized findings aggregation.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable GuardDuty: aws guardduty create-detector --enable --finding-publishing-frequency FIFTEEN_MINUTES
- Enable S3 protection: aws guardduty update-detector --detector-id <id> --data-sources S3Logs={Enable=true}
- Enable Security Hub: aws securityhub enable-security-hub --enable-default-standards
- Subscribe to findings via SNS: aws securityhub create-action-target --name notify --description 'Send to SNS'
- Set up EventBridge rule for high-severity findings: aws events put-rule --name critical-findings --event-pattern '{"source":["aws.securityhub"]}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Review audit records for inappropriate activity

controls.title: Audit Record Reduction and Report Generation

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Provide and implement audit record reduction and report generation capability.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Use CloudWatch Logs Insights and Athena for log analysis and reporting.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Set up CloudWatch Logs Insights queries for common audit scenarios
- Create Athena table for CloudTrail logs: CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE cloudtrail_logs ...
- Schedule regular audit reports via AWS Lambda and S3

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify audit record reduction and report generation capability

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Use internal system clocks to generate time stamps for audit records.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: AWS services use NTP automatically. Verify EC2 instances use Amazon Time Sync Service.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Verify Amazon Time Sync is configured on EC2: chronyc tracking (should show 169.254.169.123)
- For ECS/EKS: time sync is inherited from the host
- CloudTrail timestamps are UTC and automatically synchronized

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify time synchronization for audit records

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Protect audit information and audit logging tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable S3 Object Lock, versioning, and restrict access to CloudTrail log buckets.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable versioning: aws s3api put-bucket-versioning --bucket <trail-bucket> --versioning-configuration Status=Enabled
- Enable Object Lock: aws s3api put-object-lock-configuration --bucket <bucket> --object-lock-configuration '{"ObjectLockEnabled":"Enabled","Rule":{"DefaultRetention":{"Mode":"COMPLIANCE","Days":365}}}'
- Restrict bucket policy to CloudTrail service principal only
- Enable access logging on the trail bucket: aws s3api put-bucket-logging

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:

- Verify log file integrity: aws cloudtrail validate-logs --trail-arn <trail-arn> --start-time 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z
- Configure S3 Object Lock for CloudTrail bucket: aws s3api put-object-lock-configuration --bucket <trail-bucket> --object-lock-configuration '{"ObjectLockEnabled":"Enabled","Rule":{"DefaultRetention":{"Mode":"COMPLIANCE","Days":365}}}'
- Enable CloudTrail Insights for anomaly detection: aws cloudtrail put-insight-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> --insight-selectors '[{"InsightType":"ApiCallRateInsight"},{"InsightType":"ApiErrorRateInsight"}]'

- Check for public S3 objects: aws s3api list-buckets --query 'Buckets[].Name' | xargs -I {} aws s3api get-bucket-acl --bucket {}
- Review Access Analyzer external access findings: aws accessanalyzer list-findings --analyzer-arn <arn> --filter '{"isPublic":{"eq":["true"]}}'
